title: tabernaemontana celastroides
intro: Tabernaemontana celastroides, also known as the Philippine vine or tree of life, is a species of flowering plant in the family Apocynaceae, native to the tropical forests of the Philippines. This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including its antiseptic, anti-inflammatory, and antispasmodic properties. The health benefits of Tabernaemontana celastroides include its ability to treat fever, cough, and respiratory issues, as well as its potential to lower blood pressure and improve cardiovascular health. Herbal preparations of the plant include the use of its leaves and roots to make teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are often used to treat various ailments and promote overall well-being.
